title: Repo-Directed Improvement Evidence Threshold
goal: Add one explicit repeated-evidence threshold for when friction should remain a workspace-self-adaptation problem versus when it is justified to promote repo-directed improvement.
non_goals: Reopen the completed workspace-self-adaptation split and friction-response-order lane wholesale.
Build a scoring system, scheduler policy, or approval workflow for repo-directed change.
Ban repo improvement when repeated shared evidence clearly points to a repo-owned problem.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: keep Agentic Workspace responsible for reducing its own fit friction first while requiring stronger proof before repo-directed change is proposed or taken.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice should enable: defaults/reporting can answer not only that workspace adaptation comes first, but also what minimum evidence must exist before repo-directed improvement is justified.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: any future tuning of evidence classes or promotion heuristics beyond the compact repeated-evidence rule.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: the old contract language was still too permissive because `balanced` could be read as allowing repo-directed action from one evidence signal.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: defaults/report output now expose the repeated-evidence threshold explicitly, the improvement-latitude modes no longer imply that one signal is enough, and the doctrine/config/report docs align with that guardrail.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none for this bounded slice beyond normal future dogfooding.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: none unless future dogfooding shows the threshold is still too vague.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: implement issue `#180` as a bounded guardrail follow-on, not as a new broad friction-policy lane.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: keep workspace-self-adaptation and repo-directed improvement distinct; require repeated shared evidence rather than agent preference alone; avoid adding heavy workflow machinery.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact field names, whether the threshold is projected in defaults only or also in report output, and which existing examples best illustrate honest adaptation versus premature repo surgery.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the change would require new editable state, a generic scoring system, or a broader rework of completed self-adaptation semantics.

active_milestone.scope: freeze the repeated-evidence threshold in defaults/report payloads, align doctrine/config/report docs, and cover it with focused CLI tests.

invariants: Workspace-self-adaptation stays allowed under every improvement-latitude mode.
Repo-directed improvement requires stronger proof than local agent discomfort or one-off taste.
The new threshold must stay compact, inspectable, and subordinate to existing ownership/proof boundaries.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Repo-directed improvement should require repeated shared evidence that the repo, not the workspace, is the real friction source.
One-off agent discomfort or local preference is not enough evidence for repo-directed improvement.
Honest workspace adaptation may continue while evidence accumulates, but it must not become concealment of repeated repo-owned friction.
completion_criteria: The defaults payload exposes an explicit repeated-evidence threshold for repo-directed improvement.
The workspace report projects the same threshold in its repo-friction contract.
The design-principles, workspace-config, and reporting docs all state the same rule.
Focused workspace CLI tests prove the new fields and semantics.
`ROADMAP.md` and issue `#180` reflect the completed bounded follow-on.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: added an explicit `repo_directed_improvement_threshold` contract to `agentic-workspace defaults --section improvement_latitude --format json` and `agentic-workspace report --target ./repo --format json`, tightened the mode language so one evidence signal is no longer enough for repo-directed action, and aligned the design-principles, workspace-config, and reporting docs to the same repeated-evidence rule.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q`; `uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section improvement_latitude --format json`; `uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json`.
execution_summary.resume from: the next roadmap lane when active capacity opens; do not reopen this slice unless future dogfooding shows the repeated-evidence threshold is still too weak or ambiguous.

drift_log: 2026-04-18: Promoted issue `#180` from `ROADMAP.md` as a bounded guardrail follow-on to the completed workspace-self-adaptation lane.
2026-04-18: Completed after freezing the repeated shared-evidence threshold in defaults/report output, aligning the docs, and proving the new contract through focused workspace CLI tests.
